questions.q: Paragraph: Consider, $f(x)=\left|\sin \left(\left(2 r_1-1\right) \frac{\pi}{6}\right) x\right|+\left|\cos \left(\frac{r_2 \pi}{6}\right) x\right|$. Let a fair dice is thrown twice and $r_1, r_2$ are the numbers obtained on the dice its first and second throw respectively.Question: The probability that $f(2)$ is irrational, is:
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A certain college student has to complete his Chemistry problem set and Maths problem set on the night of December $31^{\text {st }} 2009$ (both due on $1^{\text {st }}$ January 2010). If the student works on his problem sets in the maths library, the probability of finishing his maths problem set that night is $95 \%$ and the probability of finishing his chemistry problem set that night is $75 \%$. If the student works on his problem sets in the chemistry library, the probability of finishing his chemistry problem set that night is $90 \%$ and probability of finishing his maths problem set is $80 \%$. Given that he can only work in one of the 2 libraries with $60 \%$ probability of working in maths library.Question: Given that he finishes his both problem set, the probability that he works in the maths library, is
questions.solution:
$M$ : He works in maths library C : He works in chemistry library F : He finishes his both problem set. $P(M)=\frac{60}{100}=\frac{6}{10}$
$P(C)=\frac{40}{100}=\frac{4}{10}$ $P(F / M)==\frac{P(F \cap M)}{P(M)}=\frac{\frac{60}{100} \times \frac{95}{100} \times \frac{75}{100}}{\frac{60}{100}}=\frac{95}{100} \times \frac{75}{100}=\frac{7125}{10000}=\frac{57}{80} \text { Ans.(i) }$ (By multiplication rule) $\begin{aligned} & P(F / C)=\frac{\frac{40}{100} \times \frac{90}{100} \times \frac{80}{100}}{\frac{40}{100}}=\frac{72}{100}=\frac{18}{25} \text { Ans.(ii) } \\ & P(F)=P(C \cap F)+P(M \cap F)=P(C) P(F / C)+P(M) P(F / M)=\left(\frac{4}{10} \times \frac{18}{25}\right)+\left(\frac{6}{10} \times \frac{57}{80}\right)=\frac{32}{250}+\frac{171}{400} \end{aligned}$ (By using total law of porbability) $P(M / F)=\frac{P(M) P(F / M)}{P(F)}=\frac{P(M) P(F / M)}{P(M) P(F / M)+P(C) P(F / C)}=\frac{\frac{6}{10} \times \frac{57}{80}}{\frac{6}{10} \times \frac{57}{80}+\frac{4}{10} \times \frac{18}{25}}=\frac{95}{159} \text { Ans. }$ (By using multiplication rule)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Continuous probability distributions, a continuous distribution is one in which the variate may take any value between certain limits a and b , $\mathrm{a} \lt \mathrm{b}$. Suppose that the probability of the variate X falling in the infinitesimal interval $x-\frac{1}{2} d x$ to $x+\frac{1}{2} d x$ is expressible as $f(x) d x$, where $f(x)$ is a continuous function of $x$ Symbolically, $P\left(x-\frac{1}{2} d x \leq X \leq x+\frac{1}{2} d x\right)=f(x) d x$ where $f(x)$ is called the probability density function (abbreviated as p.d.f.) or simply density function. The continuous curve $y=f(x)$ is called probability curve; and when this is symmetrical, the distribution is said to be symmetrical. Clearly, the probability density function possesses the following properties (i) $f(x) \geq 0$ for every $x$ in the interval $[a, b], a \lt b$ (ii) $\int_a^b f(x) d x=1, a, b\gt0$ since the total area under the curve is unity (iii) Furthermore, we define for any [c, d], where $c, d \in[a, b], c \lt d$; $P(c \leq X \leq d)=\int_c^d f(x) d x$ ....(1) We define $F(x)$, the cumulative distribution function (abbreviated as c.d.f.) of the random variate $X$ where $F(x)=P(X \leq x)$ or $F(x)=\int_a^x f(x) d x$ ....(2)Question: If $f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{cc}2 x ; & 0 \leq x \leq 1 \\ 0 ; & x\gt1\end{array}\right.$ then the probability that $x \leq \frac{1}{2}$ is
questions.solution: $P\left(x \leq \frac{1}{2}\right)=\int_{x=0}^{1 / 2} f(x) d x=\int_0^{1 / 2} 2 x d x=\left[x^2\right]_0^{1 / 2}=\frac{1}{4}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: A certain college student has to complete his Chemistry problem set and Maths problem set on the night of December $31^{\text {st }} 2009$ (both due on $1^{\text {st }}$ January 2010). If the student works on his problem sets in the maths library, the probability of finishing his maths problem set that night is $95 \%$ and the probability of finishing his chemistry problem set that night is $75 \%$. If the student works on his problem sets in the chemistry library, the probability of finishing his chemistry problem set that night is $90 \%$ and probability of finishing his maths problem set is $80 \%$. Given that he can only work in one of the 2 libraries with $60 \%$ probability of working in maths library.Question: Given that he works in the chemistry library, the probability that he finishes his both problem set, is
questions.solution:
$M$ : He works in maths library C : He works in chemistry library F : He finishes his both problem set. $P(M)=\frac{60}{100}=\frac{6}{10}$
$P(C)=\frac{40}{100}=\frac{4}{10}$ $P(F / M)==\frac{P(F \cap M)}{P(M)}=\frac{\frac{60}{100} \times \frac{95}{100} \times \frac{75}{100}}{\frac{60}{100}}=\frac{95}{100} \times \frac{75}{100}=\frac{7125}{10000}=\frac{57}{80} \text { Ans.(i) }$ (By multiplication rule) $\begin{aligned} & P(F / C)=\frac{\frac{40}{100} \times \frac{90}{100} \times \frac{80}{100}}{\frac{40}{100}}=\frac{72}{100}=\frac{18}{25} \text { Ans.(ii) } \\ & P(F)=P(C \cap F)+P(M \cap F)=P(C) P(F / C)+P(M) P(F / M)=\left(\frac{4}{10} \times \frac{18}{25}\right)+\left(\frac{6}{10} \times \frac{57}{80}\right)=\frac{32}{250}+\frac{171}{400} \end{aligned}$ (By using total law of porbability) $P(M / F)=\frac{P(M) P(F / M)}{P(F)}=\frac{P(M) P(F / M)}{P(M) P(F / M)+P(C) P(F / C)}=\frac{\frac{6}{10} \times \frac{57}{80}}{\frac{6}{10} \times \frac{57}{80}+\frac{4}{10} \times \frac{18}{25}}=\frac{95}{159} \text { Ans. }$ (By using multiplication rule)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A JEE aspirant estimates that she will be successful with an $80 \%$ chance if she studies 10 hours per day, with a $60 \%$ chance if she studies 7 hours per day and with a $40 \%$ chance if she studies 4 hours per day. She further believes that she will study 10 hours, 7 hours and 4 hours per day with probabilities $0.1,0.2$ and 0.7 respectively.Question: The change she will be successful is
questions.solution:
A: She gets success
T : She studies 10 hours; $\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{T})=0.1$
S : She studies 7 hours; $\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{S})=0.2$
F : She studies 4 hours: $\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{F})=0.7$
$\begin{aligned} & P\left(\frac{A}{T}\right)=0.8 ; P\left(\frac{A}{S}\right)=0.6 ; P\left(\frac{A}{F}\right)=0.4 \\ & P(A)=P(A \cap T)+P(A \cap S)+P(A \cap F) \\ & =0.48\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A JEE aspirant estimates that she will be successful with an $80 \%$ chance if she studies 10 hours per day, with a $60 \%$ chance if she studies 7 hours per day and with a $40 \%$ chance if she studies 4 hours per day. She further believes that she will study 10 hours, 7 hours and 4 hours per day with probabilities $0.1,0.2$ and 0.7 respectively.Question: Given that she is successful, the chance that she studied for 4 hours is
questions.solution:
A: She gets success
T : She studies 10 hours; $\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{T})=0.1$
S : She studies 7 hours; $\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{S})=0.2$
F : She studies 4 hours: $\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{F})=0.7$
$P\left(\frac{F}{A}\right)=\frac{P(F \cap A)}{P(A)}=\frac{7}{12}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A JEE aspirant estimates that she will be successful with an $80 \%$ chance if she studies 10 hours per day, with a $60 \%$ chance if she studies 7 hours per day and with a $40 \%$ chance if she studies 4 hours per day. She further believes that she will study 10 hours, 7 hours and 4 hours per day with probabilities $0.1,0.2$ and 0.7 respectively.Question: Given that she does not achieve success, the chance she studied for 4 hours is
questions.solution:
A: She gets success
T : She studies 10 hours; $\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{T})=0.1$
S : She studies 7 hours; $\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{S})=0.2$
F : She studies 4 hours: $\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{F})=0.7$
$P\left(\frac{F}{\bar{A}}\right)=\frac{P(F \cap \bar{A})}{P(\bar{A})}=\frac{21}{26}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Continuous probability distributions, a continuous distribution is one in which the variate may take any value between certain limits a and b , $\mathrm{a} \lt \mathrm{b}$. Suppose that the probability of the variate X falling in the infinitesimal interval $x-\frac{1}{2} d x$ to $x+\frac{1}{2} d x$ is expressible as $f(x) d x$, where $f(x)$ is a continuous function of $x$ Symbolically, $P\left(x-\frac{1}{2} d x \leq X \leq x+\frac{1}{2} d x\right)=f(x) d x$ where $f(x)$ is called the probability density function (abbreviated as p.d.f.) or simply density function. The continuous curve $y=f(x)$ is called probability curve; and when this is symmetrical, the distribution is said to be symmetrical. Clearly, the probability density function possesses the following properties (i) $f(x) \geq 0$ for every $x$ in the interval $[a, b], a \lt b$ (ii) $\int_a^b f(x) d x=1, a, b\gt0$ since the total area under the curve is unity (iii) Furthermore, we define for any [c, d], where $c, d \in[a, b], c \lt d$; $P(c \leq X \leq d)=\int_c^d f(x) d x$ ....(1) We define $F(x)$, the cumulative distribution function (abbreviated as c.d.f.) of the random variate $X$ where $F(x)=P(X \leq x)$ or $F(x)=\int_a^x f(x) d x$ ....(2)Question: Suppose the life in hours $(\mathrm{x})$ of a certain kind of radio tube has the probability density function $f(x)=\frac{100}{x^2}$ when $x\gt100$ and zero when $x \lt 100$. Then the probability that none of three such tubes in a given radio set will have to be replaced during the first 150 hours of operation, is
questions.solution:
Probability that life of a radio tube is less than or equal to 150 hours
$=\int_0^{150} f(x) d x=\int_{100}^{150} \frac{100}{x^2} d x=-\left.\frac{100}{x}\right|_{100} ^{150}=-100\left[\frac{1}{150}-\frac{1}{100}\right]=\frac{1}{3}$
$\therefore$ Probability that life is greater than $150 \mathrm{hrs}=\frac{2}{3}$
$\therefore$ Probability that none of these have life less than on equal to $150 \mathrm{hrs}=\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{2}{3} \times \frac{2}{3}=\frac{8}{27} \mathrm{~h}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
